PanelEnsembleXGBoost
  • Benchmark Comparison
  • View page source

Benchmark Comparison

The package includes a comprehensive benchmark suite to compare PanelEnsembleXGBoost against traditional panel models and modern ensemble methods.

Datasets

Three classic panel datasets are used:

Dataset

N

Groups

Periods

Dependent

Features

Grunfeld

220

11 firms

20 years

invest

value, capital

WagePanel

3815

545 workers

7 years

lwage

educ, exper, union, married

CrimeData

800

50 states

16 years

crmrte

polpc, unem

Baseline Models

  • PooledOLS: Ordinary least squares ignoring panel structure

  • PanelOLS_FE: Entity fixed effects

  • RandomEffects: Random effects model

  • MixedLM: Linear mixed model (statsmodels)

  • GPBoost: Gradient boosting with random effects

Data Split

The benchmark uses a time-based split: the first 70% of time periods are used for training, the remaining 30% for testing. This avoids the forward-looking bias that would result from random splitting of panel data.

from examples.classic_panel_benchmark import train_test_split_by_time
train, test = train_test_split_by_time(df, time_col='year', train_frac=0.7)

Running the Benchmark

cd examples
python classic_panel_benchmark.py
